title: ctenopteris pseudoarticulata
intro: Ctenopteris pseudoarticulata, also known as the New Zealand maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ctenopteris pseudoarticulata include its ability to reduce coughing and congestion, making it a valuable treatment for respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
